verdict.decider: NO supervisor/boot/cleanup path stops or fences the ingest worker before closeDb
verdict.keyCorrection: iter-005 alternativeExplanation FALSIFIED: indexSingleFile uses requireDb() which resolves to vectorIndex.getDb = `get_db as getDb` alias = initialize_db() reopen path (not the non-reopening tryGetDb); the if(!db)throw guard is dead because getDb never returns null. Reopen risk is the live path, strengthening (not weakening) the finding.
verdict.failureTrace: SIGTERM -> fatalShutdown sets private shuttingDown (cs:1560) + runs cleanup list (cs:1563-1610, NO job-queue stop) -> worker keeps running (cannot read shuttingDown; not in list) -> close_db TRUNCATEs+closes+removes marker+db=null (vis:1644-1663) -> live worker's requireDb()->getDb()->get_db()->initialize_db() bypasses `if(db&&!custom_path)return db` (vis:1492-1494 since db=null), opens new Database (vis:1520), re-sets WAL (vis:1554), re-writes .unclean-shutdown marker (vis:1562->772-787) -> fresh WAL frames written with NO subsequent TRUNCATE -> at rest: dirty marker + non-empty WAL = the exact corruption window close_db closes. Tail variant: drains starve past SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS=5000 (cs:1546) -> Promise.race force-exit (cs:1613-1628) -> close_db skipped -> also dirty.
evidence: context-server.ts:1563-1610 (cleanup list has NO job-queue/drainQueue/worker stop)
context-server.ts:147 (imports only initIngestJobQueue; no stop fn in scope)
context-server.ts:1537,1560 (shuttingDown is module-private, unreadable by job-queue)
job-queue.ts:694-722 (drainQueue loop guards only workerActive; NO shuttingDown/abort/signal guard)
vector-index-store.ts:1975 (export { get_db as getDb }) + 1976 (try_get_db as tryGetDb)
vector-index-store.ts:1491-1494 (initialize_db short-circuit only when db!=null) + 1520 (new Database) + 1554 (WAL) + 1562 (write_unclean_shutdown_marker)
verdict.mitigant: crash recovery: initIngestJobQueue -> resetIncompleteJobsToQueued re-enqueues incomplete jobs (job-queue.ts:765-768); boot reads dirty marker + repairs => recoverable durability/consistency regression, not unrecoverable data loss => caps at P1
verdict.skepticPass: Hunter found no fence (6 candidates checked). Skeptic's 4 counters all refuted: (1) requireDb-throws-safely FALSE (it reopens); (2) deadline-force-kill-first => also dirty exit, not a refutation; (3) jobs-idle-in-practice => lowers frequency not validity, operationally reachable via shared daemon; (4) data-loss-vs-recoverable => recoverable via crash recovery. Referee: CONFIRMED P1, downgradeTrigger not met.
